name: Public Surface Build — turn invisible work into compounding work
status: declared — build order set, Twitter is first move
next_move: Resurrect Twitter (lowest friction, existing 1,400-follower audience). First concrete step: one post reopening the channel — framework-adjacent, not a cold restart announcement. Use dormant-but-not-dead positioning; act as if the audience is already there (because it is).
blockers: Community scouting unresolved — 'where do people like me congregate' is an explicit open question (big-ideas builders who are actually shipping: Discords? subreddits? indie-founder forums? research-adjacent communities?)
No LinkedIn presence to speak of — needs build before partnership gets serious, not after
YouTube production cost not scoped — video is highest-bandwidth but also highest-friction; wait until the other three flow
live_band: Twitter → community scouting → LinkedIn → YouTube. Twitter is friction-minimum, community scouting is intel-generation, LinkedIn is credibility-infrastructure, YouTube is bandwidth-maximum. Don't reorder — each step's existence de-risks the next. The anchor framing: the framework is not fully alive until it has a public surface. Right now everything is brilliant and invisible. Public surface is what turns invisible work into compounding work.

twitter.next_move: One reopening post that behaves as if the audience is still there. Framework-adjacent, not meta-announcement.

community_scouting.positioning: needs scouting — where do builders-who-ship big ideas actually congregate?
community_scouting.next_move: Scout Discords, indie-dev/founder forums, research-adjacent communities, specific subreddits. Produce a ranked short-list — don't join everything, pick 2-3.

linkedin.positioning: credibility infrastructure — needs to exist before the partnership play escalates
linkedin.next_move: Profile populate pass first — before any content. Get the surface credible, then post onto it.

youtube.positioning: highest-bandwidth channel for showing what Mission Control / Secretary / the framework actually does; highest production cost
youtube.next_move: Wait until Twitter + community + LinkedIn are flowing. Don't front-load production cost.

notes: Build order is load-bearing. Twitter first not because it's the most important but because it's the lowest-friction move with the highest immediate yield — 1,400 followers is latent heat. LinkedIn before partnership escalates is a timing constraint, not a preference. YouTube last is about sequencing production investment. The whole project is rhythm-shaped: public surfaces don't earn compounding through a single launch, they earn it through sustained presence. Anchor weight is medium (0.65) deliberately — this shouldn't outrank the core build work on any given day.
